symbols: Mephistopheles in red — the tempter and deceiver, a literary figure echoing the biblical Satan
Marguerite in white surrounded by flowers — purity and innocence targeted by evil
skeleton at Mephistopheles' feet — death as the wages of sin (Romans 6:23) in literary form
gothic arches — the corruption of sacred space by demonic presence
Faust's dark attire — the soul in darkness, having bargained away its eternal destiny
description: A triptych-format painting in three vertical panels. The left panel shows Mephistopheles as a red-robed, sinister figure hunched over a book beside an old man (Faust), with a skeleton visible at their feet and a gothic window behind. The central panel presents Marguerite as a young woman in a white dress adorned with flowers, standing amid a garden of blossoms and roses. The right panel depicts a dark, tall figure in black (Faust or another manifestation of Mephistopheles) standing near gothic architectural elements.
